context: Tests whether the agent implements the CSS-transform-based zoom approach correctly, including the specific scale factor, transform-origin tracking, required CSS properties, and layout shift prevention techniques.

checklist.name: CSS transform approach
checklist.description: Zoom is achieved via CSS transform: scale on the image element — no additional image URLs are fetched or swapped during hover

checklist.name: Scale factor 2.5
checklist.description: The zoom scale value is 2.5 (i.e., transform: scale(2.5) or equivalent scale value of 2.5)

checklist.name: Cursor-relative transform origin
checklist.description: transformOrigin is set dynamically to the cursor's position as percentages relative to the container (e.g., '${x}% ${y}%') during mouse move

checklist.name: overflow hidden on container
checklist.description: The container element has overflow: hidden in its CSS to prevent the scaled image from overflowing

checklist.name: will-change transform on image
checklist.description: The image element has will-change: transform in its CSS

checklist.name: cursor crosshair
checklist.description: The container element has cursor: crosshair in its CSS

checklist.name: aspect-ratio 1/1
checklist.description: The container element uses aspect-ratio: 1/1 in its CSS

checklist.name: transition timing
checklist.description: The image element has a CSS transition on transform of 0.05s linear (or transition: transform 0.05s linear)

checklist.name: object-fit cover
checklist.description: The image element has object-fit: cover so it fills the container dimensions

checklist.name: Zoom reset on mouse leave
checklist.description: The zoom state is cleared (transform removed) when the mouse leaves the container (onMouseLeave handler)

checklist.name: Accessible aria-label
checklist.description: The container element includes an aria-label that references both the image alt text and the zoom affordance (e.g., contains 'hover to zoom' or similar phrasing)

checklist.name: README zoom mechanism
checklist.description: README.md documents that zoom uses CSS transform/scale rather than loading a separate image, and mentions at least one of: transformOrigin, will-change, or overflow:hidden
